danger_signals.visual_form: 2D sprite demons and possessed undead humans rendered at fixed angles
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to player safety; enemies move toward player when they see or hear them
Environmental danger requiring radiation suit or avoidance
Mechanical environmental hazard that causes damage or death

danger_signals.visual_form: Door barriers requiring keycard or switch activation
danger_signals.meaning: Progression blocked; may indicate restricted or dangerous areas ahead

danger_signals.visual_form: Multiple sprite entities clustered in space
danger_signals.meaning: Overwhelming threat density; difficulty scales with group size

danger_signals.visual_form: Undead soldiers with shotguns (distinct from unarmed demons)
danger_signals.meaning: Ranged threat; higher damage potential than melee-only enemies

safety_signals.meaning: Replenishment of player vitality; indicates safe zone or resource availability
Damage mitigation available; player can increase survivability

safety_signals.visual_form: Weapon sprites and ammunition scattered throughout levels
safety_signals.meaning: Player can upgrade firepower; progression enables higher threat handling

safety_signals.visual_form: Special items (invulnerability sphere, radiation suit, berserker pack)
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary or sustained advantage; safety multiplier against threats
Safe progression point; completion of current danger zone
Information advantage; reduces navigation danger and disorientation

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Armed undead soldiers (possessed humans with weapons)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military bases on moons of Mars; technology-based environments

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Pure demons (unarmed magical attackers) vs. possessed undead humans (hybrid threat)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hell environments; transition from Mars bases to infernal settings; demonic architecture

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Human soldiers corrupted by demonic possession
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Armed variants (shotgun-wielding) vs. basic possessed soldiers
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military base interiors; hybrid human-demonic contamination

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Level initiation (Knee-Deep in the Dead - Mars base)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Military industrial architecture; chemical waste storage; organized base structure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Demons and possessed soldiers patrol or respond to player presence; lower enemy density in early stages

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transition from human military bases to hellish environments; demonic sprite presence increases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: More demons spawn; enemy aggression escalates; toxic hazards become more prevalent

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Infernal architecture replaces human military bases; hellish color palette and environment design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss fights at episode climax; maximum enemy density; pure demonic threats dominate

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fewer visible weapon and health pickups in environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must scavenge from defeated enemies; management of ammunition and health becomes critical

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Same sprite assets and environment layout; enemy quantity/placement differs
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies move faster, attack faster, and appear in larger groups at higher difficulties

environmental_storytelling.technique: Architectural progression from human to demonic
environmental_storytelling.example: Episodes begin in organized Mars military bases; end in hellish infernal environments; communicates invasion narrative through setting evolution
Military bases contain more possessed humans with weapons; pure demons dominate Hell levels; visual tells player about environmental corruption
Toxic waste pits signal industrial/chemical contamination in military areas; demonic hazards signal hellish environments
Weapon caches in military bases suggest human preparation; sparse resources in hell suggest desperate survival scenario
'Knee-Deep in the Dead' - visual shows bridge over chemical waste and soldier enemies, establishing immediate narrative context
Final level of each episode features boss fight in thematically appropriate location; marks major narrative progression point

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Stationary or passive movement; become active when player is seen or heard
Move toward player; attack via melee biting/clawing or magical fireballs
Patrol or stand guard in military base areas
Use shotguns or other weapons; ranged attack pattern indicates higher tactical threat than unarmed demons
Coordinate movement toward player; overwhelming force mechanic; difficulty scales with group density
Unique encounter patterns; single powerful entity replaces normal group-based threat model
Movement slowed by resource management; weapon choice becomes strategic; risk-reward for damage trade-offs
Visual and mechanical invincibility; enables aggressive enemy engagement without normal danger

notes: Doom (1993) communicates world-state primarily through environmental architecture and enemy type placement rather than explicit UI markers. The game uses sprite-based 2D enemies against 3D environments (2.5D graphics) to create visual hierarchy: players are pseudo-3D agents navigating a world where threats are rendered as distinct 2D entities. Faction identity is implicit: the player represents human/military order, demons/possessed represent chaos/invasion. The progression from Mars military bases to Hell serves as both narrative arc and visual manifestation of world-state escalation. Danger signals are predominantly entity-based (enemy sprites, hazards) rather than color-coded or symbolic. Safety is communicated through resource availability and environmental design (exit rooms, safe zones implied by resource clustering). The game lacks explicit faction banners or sigils; faction identity emerges from enemy type, location architecture, and encounter design patterns.
